title: Wild About
subTitle: Lizards have existed here long before us; surviving natural disasters and mass extinctions, they are now among the oldest animals on Earth.

title: Wild About
subTitle: Bears are divided into eight species with some considered the largest land predators, though many are in fact omnivorous; they survive in our grasslands and mountains, tropical jungles and the cold harsh climate of the Arctic.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: Animals adapt through a series of small and random mutations to compete more effectively for food, water and space. Certain creatures have gone through different processes in order to evolve into what they are today.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: Techniques big cats use to help them win the race of life.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at how desert animals have evolved to withstand the extreme heat and lack of water, while exploring the history of scorpions.

subTitle: The elephants look for grazing land in the middle of a drought which brings them into conflict with the Maasai farmers.

subTitle: Big changes are in store for PawsAbility as Janice moves her practice; an old friend brings new visitors for brace fittings; a former Olympic Sledge Hockey champion and his miniature dachshund get much needed cart adjustments.

subTitle: Janice is being kept busy with a rare case of a dog who uses prosthetics on both front legs.

subTitle: Things are getting tiny as Janice attempts to create a prosthetic leg for a Chihuahua; a talkative black Lab who loves to chase squirrels and an Akita with an autoimmune disease need specialized braces.

subTitle: A spoiled pup from Delaware needs a brace to fix an injured back leg; a cadaver dog gets a brace to keep working.

subTitle: At a rescue center in Pennsylvania a dog gets a brace to help it walk; a dog named Rigby is healing from a torn Achilles.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage, Alaska, is home to hundreds of black bears; it's these black bears, that are patrolling the city; with their brains, their brawn and their cunning, these bears have mastered life in the urban jungle.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage has a resident who is renowned as nature's lumberjack and North America's largest rodent, the beaver; with their powerful teeth, the trees they fell can be huge and their dams can be seen from space.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage is home to a huge number of wild porcupines, living in the city's parks, gardens and streets; almost twice the average size, Anchorage's porcupines search for food in the city and find refuge during Alaskan winters.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Travelling 3500 miles through rivers and streams, salmon are Alaska's hardiest resident; there is one hazard they face right at the end of their journey; it's the brown bears.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: The Dog Dudes are surprised when they get a call from a widower about an uncontrollable Labrador wreaking havoc in her life. Usually a tame breed, Hugo became a big problem when his human leader passed away unexpectedly three years ago.

title: Super Senses: Special Forces
subTitle: The mantis shrimp's visual systems; the owl's hearing; the extensive sensorimotor specializations of the elephant's trunk.

title: Special Forces
subTitle: In the water, it takes sophisticated navigation systems, fine-tuned reflexes, and high-precision detection methods to survive. These animals have what it takes.

title: Super Senses: Special Forces
subTitle: Sight, hearing, smell, touch, taste and beyond; survival demands an edge; these animals' abilities qualify them for nature's special forces.

subTitle: The electric catfish and electric ray use shocks to attack their victims, while elephants and kangaroos fight with their feet.
